zh.Haverhill, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可哈維爾爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Roger Myer Turner於1344年出生在英國素福爾可哈維爾爾時，他的父親Roger Turner24歲，母親Christina de Chalfunte23歲。他與Christina De Chalfhunte在英國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1386年在英國享年42歲。

zh-hans.Haverhill, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可哈維爾爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Roger Myer Turner于1344年出生在英国素福爾可哈維爾爾时，他的父亲Roger Turner24岁，母亲Christina de Chalfunte23岁。他与Christina De Chalfhunte在英国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1386年在英国享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: Turner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English t(o)urnour, turner ‘turner’ (Old French to(u)rn(e)our), mainly denoting someone who fashioned small objects of wood, metal, or bone on a lathe, but also a variety of other occupations, including turnspit and translator or interpreter. This surname may have become confused with Toner . In North America, it is also very common among African Americans.

English: occasionally perhaps a nickname from Middle English turn-hare, a compound of Middle English tournen ‘to turn, direct, steer’ + hare ‘hare’, a name for someone in charge of the greyhounds in hare coursing or an exaggerated compliment for someone who could run fast. See also Turnbull .
English: perhaps also from Middle English t(o)urn(e)our ‘jouster, one who takes part in a tournament’ (Old French tornoieor, tournoieur).

variants.name: Trinder
meanings: English (Oxfordshire and Gloucestershire): occupational name from a derivative of Middle English trenden ‘to turn, roll, twist’ (Old English trendan), denoting someone who turned something such as wood or fibres. Compare Turner , Spinner .

variants.name: Toner
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tomhrair ‘descendant of Tomhrar’, a Gaelicized form of the Norse personal name Thorr. Compare Thunder .
English: nickname from Old French toner ‘thunder’, perhaps for someone with a thunderous voice. Compare Thunder .
English: variant of   Towner  .

variants.name: Tarner
meanings: English: variant of   Turner  . This form of the surname is relatively rare in Britain and Ireland. It may sometimes have been confused with   Taverner  .
German (North Rhine-Westphalia): unexplained. This surname is rare in Germany.

variants.name: Turney
meanings: English (of Norman origin):
habitational name from any of the places in northern France called Tournai-sur-Dive (Orne), Tournay-sur-Odon (Calvados), or Tourny (Eure), or occasionally from Tournai in Hainaut (Belgium).

perhaps occasionally an occupational name from Middle English tournei, turnai ‘lawyer’ (Anglo-Norman French tourné), or a nickname from Middle English tournei(en) ‘(to) joust, take part in a tournament’ (Old French torneier). Compare Turner .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

nameMeanings.name: Myer

meanings: traditional name, meaning ‘giving light’ in Hebrew.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Roger Myer Turner was born in 1344, in Haverhill, Suffolk, England, his father, Roger Turner, was 24 and his mother, Christina de Chalfunte, was 23. He married Christina De Chalfhunte in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1386, in England, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Roger Myer Turner est né en 1344, à Haverhill, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Roger Turner, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Christina de Chalfunte, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Christina De Chalfhunte à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1386, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Roger Myer Turner 1344 in Haverhill, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Roger Turner 24 und seine Mutter Christina de Chalfunte 23. Er hat in England Christina De Chalfhunte geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1386 im Alter von 42 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Roger Myer Turner è nato il nel 1344 nel Haverhill, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Roger Turner, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Christina de Chalfunte, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Christina De Chalfhunte nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1386, nel England, all'età di 42 anni.

ja.Haverhill, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスヘイバーヒル
ja.Haverhill, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクヘイバーヒル
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Roger Myer Turnerが1344年にイギリススフォルクヘイバーヒルで生まれた時，父Roger Turnerは24歳，母Christina de Chalfunteは23歳であった。イギリスでChristina De Chalfhunteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1386年にイギリスで42歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Roger Myer Turner(이)가 1344년 영국 수폴크 하버힐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger Turner(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina de Chalfunte(은)는 23세였다. 그는 영국에서 Christina De Chalfhunte(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1386년 영국에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Roger Myer Turner nasceu em 1344, em Haverhill, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Turner, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Christina de Chalfunte, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Christina De Chalfhunte em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1386, em Inglaterra, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Roger Myer Turner родился в 1344 г. в местности хавирилл, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Roger Turner было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Christina de Chalfunte, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Christina De Chalfhunte в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1386 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Roger Myer Turner nació en 1344, en Haverhill, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Turner, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Christina de Chalfunte, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Christina De Chalfhunte en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1386, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 42 años.
